description_long: This is a single-story bungalow with a sprawling, horizontal layout and a prominent central entrance. The roof is a complex hip design, featuring multiple intersecting sections that create a visually interesting silhouette. The facade includes a covered porch at the main entrance, supported by columns, and a series of evenly spaced rectangular windows along the front and sides. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or brick, with a shingled roof and large window openings suggesting a residential function. The building sits on a flat base, typical of suburban or urban neighborhoods, and the overall design emphasizes accessibility and comfort. The exterior is finished in a uniform, light color, giving it a clean and modern appearance.
description: A single-story, hip-roofed bungalow with a central porch, symmetrical window arrangement, and a clean, modern exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a covered porch and evenly spaced windows on either side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the building and the intersecting hip roof sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a long row of windows and the extended width of the bungalow.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows and the continuation of the hip roof design.
